title: A coin I found on the street
summary: Tell a small story about an old coin you once found on the street.
topic: A coin I found on the street
prompt: Write 6–8 short sentences about a coin you found. Say when and where, what it looked like, and what you did with it. Use past simple.
sampleText: One morning in Naples, I walked to a small bakery near my flat. The street was wet from the rain. I saw something shiny on the ground next to a wall. I bent down and I picked it up. It was a small coin. The coin was very dirty. I cleaned it on my shirt and I looked at it again. The coin was old. I could not read the year, but the number two was clear. I put the coin in my pocket and I walked home with my bread. At home, I washed the coin with soap and water. The coin was made of copper. It was brown and a little green. I do not know who lost it. Maybe it fell from a man's pocket many years ago. I keep the coin in a small box on my desk. Sometimes I take it out and I look at it. It is not worth much money, but I feel lucky every time I see it. A small object can hold a quiet story.

sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: thứ gì đó sáng bóng trên mặt đất

sampleAnnotations.phrase: put the coin in my pocket
washed the coin with soap and water
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: rửa đồng xu bằng xà phòng và nước

sampleAnnotations.phrase: keep the coin in a small box
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: giữ đồng xu trong hộp nhỏ

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Past simple with irregular verbs
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Truyện ngắn dùng quá khứ đơn. Chú ý động từ bất quy tắc: see → saw, bend → bent, put → put, take → took, fall → fell.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: A short story uses past simple. Watch irregular verbs: see → saw, bend → bent, put → put, take → took, fall → fell.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Phrasal verb 'pick up'
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'pick up' nghĩa là cúi nhặt vật gì đó. Có thể tách giữa: 'pick it up', 'pick the coin up'. Sau đại từ (it, them) bắt buộc tách.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'pick up' means to lift an object from the ground. It can be split: 'pick it up', 'pick the coin up'. With a pronoun (it, them), splitting is required.

sampleTranslationVi: Một buổi sáng ở Naples, tôi đi bộ đến một tiệm bánh nhỏ gần căn hộ. Đường còn ướt vì mưa. Tôi thấy một vật gì đó sáng bóng trên mặt đất gần một bức tường. Tôi cúi xuống và nhặt nó lên. Đó là một đồng xu nhỏ. Đồng xu rất bẩn. Tôi lau nó vào áo và nhìn lại. Đồng xu cũ. Tôi không đọc được năm, nhưng số hai thì rõ. Tôi bỏ đồng xu vào túi và đi về nhà với bánh mì. Ở nhà, tôi rửa đồng xu bằng xà phòng và nước. Đồng xu làm bằng đồng. Nó màu nâu và hơi xanh. Tôi không biết ai đã đánh rơi nó. Có thể nó rơi từ túi một người đàn ông nhiều năm trước. Tôi giữ đồng xu trong một cái hộp nhỏ trên bàn. Đôi khi tôi lấy ra và nhìn nó. Nó không đáng giá nhiều tiền, nhưng tôi cảm thấy may mắn mỗi khi nhìn nó. Một vật nhỏ có thể chứa một câu chuyện thầm lặng.

mcQuestions.prompt: Where did the writer find the coin?
options: On the street next to a wall
In a small box at home
In the rain by the river
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'I saw something shiny on the ground next to a wall'.
mcQuestions.hint: Look for the place where he saw something shiny.

mcQuestions.prompt: What was the weather that morning?
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'The street was wet from the rain'.

mcQuestions.prompt: What did the writer do first with the coin?
options: He cleaned it on his shirt and looked again.
He put it in a box.
He gave it to a friend.
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'I cleaned it on my shirt and I looked at it again'.
mcQuestions.hint: Find the action right after he picked it up.

mcQuestions.prompt: What metal was the coin made of?
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'The coin was made of copper'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Where does the writer keep the coin now?
options: In a small box on his desk
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'I keep the coin in a small box on my desk'.
mcQuestions.hint: Look near the end of the text.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which past simple form is correct?
Which sentence uses 'pick up' correctly with a pronoun?
mcQuestions.explanation: With a pronoun, 'pick up' must split: 'pick it up'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which adjective best describes the coin when he first saw it?
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'The coin was very dirty'.
mcQuestions.hint: Look at the sentence after 'It was a small coin'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Why does the writer keep the coin?
options: Because it is worth a lot of money.
Because he feels lucky every time he sees it.
Because his mother told him to.
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'I feel lucky every time I see it'.
mcQuestions.hint: Find the sentence about how he feels.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct past simple of 'put'.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'put' is irregular: the past form is also 'put'.
mcQuestions.hint: 'put' does not change in the past simple.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Why do we sometimes keep small objects with no money value? What do they really hold for us?
